name: Brainrot
white: Dad using last year's slang with total confidence.
Saying "rizz" at Thanksgiving and watching Grandpa age ten years.
Grandpa calling the doctor about a word he heard at dinner.
A word that means nothing, everything, and also "hello."
Slang so new it expired mid-sentence.
Translating for Grandma in real time, like at the United Nations.
The teacher's desk drawer full of confiscated words.
"It's giving..." as a complete medical diagnosis.
A little brother who communicates entirely in sound effects.
Mom asking, very quietly, if "cooked" is something bad.
Grandpa practicing the new word in the mirror before dinner.
Dad's forty-slide presentation on what the kids are saying.
A slang dictionary that was outdated before it was printed.
A substitute teacher using our slang as a trap.
A cursed video about a singing toilet.
Grandma saying "no cap" and refusing to reveal her sources.
The family group chat after Grandpa discovers the skull emoji.
A word so powerful the principal called an assembly.
Explaining that "mid" is an insult now.
Dad calling everything "based" for an entire road trip.
The evening news finally explaining our slang, six months too late.
A group project conducted entirely in vibes.
Whatever "Ohio" means now.
Teaching Grandpa a fake slang word and watching it spread.
A teacher who uses the slang correctly, which is somehow worse.
A birthday card from Grandma that just says "Stay sigma."
The look parents exchange when a new word enters the house.
A family meeting about the word I taught the baby.
Mom's book club spending an hour decoding a two-word text.
The dictionary people, giving up.

black.text: Grandpa heard me say _ and quietly dialed the doctor.
According to Dad, all the kids these days are really into _.
New slang just dropped: one word that somehow means _.
My teacher confiscated _ and said I can have it back after graduation.
Grandma nodded politely for ten minutes while I explained _.
This morning's school assembly was about why we all need to stop saying _.
Last month everyone was obsessed with _, but that already expired — now it's all about _.
